title: Ines and the Missing Teddy
synopsis: When Ines can't find her beloved teddy bear anywhere, she feels very sad and scared. With help from her caring family, she searches everywhere and learns that it's okay to feel worried, but problems can be solved when we work together.

pages.text: Ines loved her soft brown teddy bear. She hugged Teddy every night before bed. Teddy made her feel safe and happy. But tonight, something was wrong. Ines looked at her bed, but Teddy wasn't there!

illustration.description: A young Latina girl with curly dark hair and brown eyes stands beside her bed looking confused. She wears pink polka-dot pajamas. Her bed has colorful quilted blankets but no teddy bear. The bedroom has soft yellow walls with cheerful animal pictures and a night light glowing warmly.
illustration.alt: Ines in pajamas looking at her empty bed

pages.text: Ines felt scared and sad. Her eyes filled with tears. "Where is my Teddy?" she cried. She looked under her pillow and behind her books, but Teddy was nowhere to be found. Ines didn't know what to do.

illustration.description: Ines kneeling on the floor with tears in her eyes, lifting up her pillow and looking behind a small bookshelf. Her expression shows worry and sadness. Scattered toys and books are around her. The room has warm orange lighting from a lamp, creating gentle shadows.
illustration.alt: Ines crying while searching under pillows and behind books

pages.text: "Mama! Papa!" called Ines. Her parents came running to her room. Mama, with her long black hair in a braid, knelt down and gave Ines a big hug. Papa, wearing his favorite blue shirt, sat on the bed. "What's wrong, mija?" Mama asked gently.

illustration.description: Ines being hugged by her mother, a Latina woman with long braided black hair wearing a soft green sweater. Her father, a Latino man with short dark hair and a blue shirt, sits on the bed with a caring expression. The family is close together in the cozy bedroom with warm lighting.
illustration.alt: Ines's parents comforting her in her bedroom

pages.text: "I can't find Teddy anywhere!" Ines sniffled. "I'm scared to sleep without him." Mama wiped away her tears. "It's okay to feel scared," she said. "Let's be brave detectives and find Teddy together." Papa nodded. "We'll search everywhere!"

illustration.description: The family huddles together making a plan. Mama gently wipes Ines's tears while Papa points toward the door with determination. All three have hopeful expressions. The room is bright with cheerful yellow walls and colorful toys scattered around, showing the adventure ahead.
illustration.alt: The family making a plan to search for the missing teddy

pages.text: The family searched all over the house. They looked in the kitchen, where Abuela was making warm milk. They checked the living room, where Abuelo was reading his newspaper. "Have you seen Teddy?" asked Ines. Both grandparents shook their heads but joined the search.

illustration.description: The extended family searching together in a cozy living room. Abuela, an elderly Latina woman with gray hair in a bun and a colorful apron, looks under couch cushions. Abuelo, with silver hair and glasses, peers behind a chair. The room has warm earth tones with family photos on the walls.
illustration.alt: The whole family searching in different rooms of the house

pages.text: Finally, Papa had an idea. "Where did you play today, Ines?" he asked. Ines thought hard. "The garden!" she remembered. They all went outside with flashlights. There, under the big apple tree, was Teddy! He was a little dirty but safe.

illustration.description: The family outside at dusk with flashlights, discovering the brown teddy bear under a large apple tree. Ines reaches toward Teddy with joy and relief on her face. The garden has lush green grass and colorful flowers. The sky is deep purple with the first stars appearing, creating a magical discovery moment.
illustration.alt: The family finding Teddy under the apple tree with flashlights

pages.text: Ines hugged Teddy tight. She felt so happy and relieved! "I was scared you were lost forever," she whispered to Teddy. Mama helped brush off the leaves and dirt. "Sometimes we lose things we love, but that doesn't mean they're gone forever," Mama said.

illustration.description: Ines clutching her teddy bear with pure joy and relief, her face beaming with happiness. Mama gently brushes leaves off the teddy while Papa and the grandparents smile warmly in the background. The garden setting is peaceful with soft moonlight filtering through the apple tree leaves.
illustration.alt: Ines joyfully reunited with her clean teddy bear

pages.text: Back in her bedroom, Ines felt brave and safe again. "Thank you for helping me find Teddy," she said, giving everyone goodnight hugs. She learned that when she felt scared or sad, her family would always help her. Now she could sleep peacefully with Teddy in her arms.

illustration.description: Ines tucked in bed with her teddy bear, surrounded by her loving family giving goodnight kisses and hugs. Everyone has warm, content expressions. The bedroom glows with soft night light, and stars twinkle outside the window. The scene radiates love, safety, and peaceful resolution.
illustration.alt: Ines peacefully in bed with Teddy, surrounded by her caring family

metadata.illustrationStyle: Bright, cheerful cartoon style with expressive faces. Bold primary colors. Rounded, friendly shapes. Slightly textured backgrounds like construction paper.

questions.question: How did Ines feel when she couldn't find Teddy?
questions.explanation: Ines felt scared and sad when she couldn't find her beloved teddy bear.

questions.question: Who helped Ines look for Teddy?
questions.explanation: Ines's whole family helped search for Teddy - Mama, Papa, Abuela, and Abuelo.

questions.question: Where did they find Teddy?
questions.explanation: They found Teddy under the big apple tree in the garden where Ines had been playing.

questions.question: How did Ines feel at the end of the story?
questions.explanation: At the end, Ines felt happy and safe because she had Teddy back and knew her family would always help her.

pages.text: Ines felt _____ when she couldn't find Teddy. Her _____ came to help her search everywhere.
They found Teddy under the _____ tree. Ines felt _____ and safe again.
